verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Main blocker: Surfline consistency is lully.

conversationalSummary.text: Tomorrow is probably one to skip. Meio da Baia at Lunch looks like the least-bad option, but not really something worth chasing. The wind looks like the main issue and could easily make it messy. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam first, but unless it looks surprisingly clean, I'd save the drive.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 12 kJ, 10 s period, lully 1/100, and wind is the main risk.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 10-13s at low tide can still work on exposed beaches; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; Meio da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size; Meio da Baia may feel too soft without more push.
best.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 13 kJ, 10 s period, lully 1/100, and wind is the main risk.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 10-13s at low tide can still work on exposed beaches; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
